parts.text: Crop rotation is a common practice on Irish tillage farms. Explain the underlined term. State two advantages of crop rotation. Suggest two disadvantages of using chemical sprays in crop production.
solution: Crop rotation is a method of farming where a field is planted with a different crop each year. This practice helps in maintaining soil health, reducing pest and disease buildup, and improving soil structure. Advantages of crop rotation include improved soil fertility, reduced pest and disease resistance, and increased biodiversity. Disadvantages include increased labor and equipment requirements, and the need for diverse crop varieties.
Chemical sprays are used in crop production to control pests and diseases. Disadvantages include potential harm to the environment, resistance development, and economic costs.

subparts.text: Crop rotation is a common practice on Irish tillage farms. Explain the underlined term.
solution: Crop rotation is a method of farming where a field is planted with a different crop each year.

parts.text: (i) Define the term biological control. (ii) Give two examples of biological control on farms. (iii) Outline two advantages of using biological controls in crop production.
solution: Biological control is the use of living organisms to control pest populations.
Examples of biological control on farms include using beneficial insects, fungi, and bacteria to control pests.
Advantages of using biological controls include reduced chemical use, decreased environmental impact, and improved crop health.

subparts.text: Define the term biological control.
solution: Biological control is the use of living organisms to control pest populations.

context: A student carries out an investigation to compare the infiltration rate of two soils; a compacted soil and an uncompacted soil.

parts.text: State the independent variable the student controlled during the investigation.
solution: The independent variable the student controlled during the investigation is the type of soil (compacted or uncompacted).

parts.text: Describe how they measured the infiltration rate in this investigation.
solution: The infiltration rate can be measured by observing the amount of water that passes through the soil over a given period of time. This can be done using a simple experiment where water is poured onto the soil and the amount that seeps through is measured over a set period. Alternatively, infiltration rate can be measured using a device such as a water infiltration meter or a soil moisture sensor.

parts.text: Suggest one way in which farmers could improve the infiltration rate of their soil.
solution: One way in which farmers could improve the infiltration rate of their soil is to add organic matter such as compost or manure. This will help to create a more permeable soil structure, which will allow water to infiltrate more easily. Farmers could also consider using cover crops, which will help to reduce soil erosion and improve soil structure. Additionally, farmers could use drip irrigation, which will help to reduce water loss due to evaporation and run-off.

subparts.text: Explain why they have included an isolation area on the plan

subparts.text: Describe the purpose of the footbath

context: Read the following article on liver fluke and the questions which follow. Liver fluke thrive in the Irish wet climate. It is estimated to cost Irish farmers over €90 million annually. It is an issue in both cattle and sheep, but sheep are often the most badly affected. It affects all ages of sheep as it is picked up off the grass when sheep are grazing. This commonly occurs in the autumn and winter months. (Adapted from Teagasc, 2020) (Adapted from Animalhealthireland, 2020)

parts.text: (i) Explain notifiable disease. (ii) List two notifiable diseases found in Irish farm animals. 1. 2. (iii) Outline the importance to Irish Agriculture of farmers complying with regulations in relation to notifiable diseases. (iv) State three practices farmers could employ to ensure good biosecurity on their farm. 1. 2. 3. (v) Discuss three management practices farmers should consider when housing farm animals to ensure high standards of animal welfare.

subparts.text: Explain notifiable disease.
solution: A notifiable disease is a contagious or infectious disease which, by its nature or by reason of its incidence, is declared by the Minister for Health to be notifiable. This means that under the Animal Disease Act 1992, any person who knows or suspects that an animal has a notifiable disease must report it to the National Notifiable Disease Service.

subparts.text: List two notifiable diseases found in Irish farm animals. 1. 2.
Outline the importance to Irish Agriculture of farmers complying with regulations in relation to notifiable diseases.
solution: Compliance with regulations in relation to notifiable diseases is crucial for protecting public health and animal welfare. It ensures that diseases that could pose a threat to human health or livestock are detected and managed effectively, preventing the spread of infection and minimizing the economic impact on farmers.

subparts.text: State three practices farmers could employ to ensure good biosecurity on their farm. 1. 2. 3.
Discuss three management practices farmers should consider when housing farm animals to ensure high standards of animal welfare. 1. 2. 3.
solution: Provision of adequate space and bedding
Ensuring access to clean water and food
Regular monitoring of animal behavior and health
